rxn_class_summary: Photoelectrocatalytic dehydrogenative carbocyclization of unactivated alkene-tethered quinazolin-4-ones with diethyl malonate to give polycyclic pyrroloquinazolinones, with H2 as sole byproduct
electrochemical_role: Anodic PCET oxidation of base-activated diethyl malonate generates a malonate radical; cathode reduces protons to H2; photoexcited Ir(ppy)3* (via oxidative quenching) reoxidizes the cyclized N-centered radical to a nitranion (electron shuttle anchoring an EC intermediate)

key_intermediates.name: malonate radical (A) from PCET of diethyl malonate
alkyl radical B from addition of malonate radical to tethered alkene
N-centered cyclohexadienyl-type radical C from intramolecular amidine radical trapping
nitranion D from SET reduction of C by excited Ir(ppy)3*
tetrahydropyrroloquinazolinone E (protonated D, detected by HRMS)
Ir(ppy)3 / Ir(ppy)3* / Ir(IV) photocatalyst states (oxidative quenching cycle)

electron_flow: Base-activated diethyl malonate is oxidized at the anode via PCET to a malonate radical that adds to the tethered alkene; the resulting alkyl radical cyclizes onto the quinazolinone amidine to give an N-centered radical C, which is oxidized by photoexcited Ir(ppy)3* to a nitranion D (regenerating Ir(III) at the cathode); protonation, anodic oxidation, and deprotonation deliver the aromatized product while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Malonate–DPE adduct 5 isolated in 66%, implicating malonate radical

probe_reagents.name: IrCl4 (as alternative oxidant)
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without current, ruling out Ir(IV) as key oxidant of substrate (path c)

probe_reagents.name: Electrochemically generated Ir(IV) (no light control)
probe_reagents.observation: Failed to promote carbocyclization without electricity, ruling out oxidative quenching shuttle (path b)

probe_reagents.name: UVA LEDs (365/385 nm, no PC)
probe_reagents.observation: Only 35–38% (vs 36% dark) — no triplet sensitization of 1b operative (path d excluded)

notes: Authors explicitly rule out four alternative pathways: (a) reductive quenching photocatalytic cycle alone, (b) Ir(IV) as electron shuttle between anode and substrate, (c) highly oxidized excited-state Ir(IV)* acting as photooxidant, and (d) energy-transfer triplet sensitization of the quinazolinone 1b. Favored proposal is anodic generation of malonate radical (paths f/g) with Ir(ppy)3 oxidative quenching cycle reoxidizing the cyclohexadienyl N-radical C to nitranion D. H2 evolution at cathode confirmed by detection experiments (Fig. S24). Intermediate E detected by HRMS.
